original_image_tags: A detailed, hand-drawn map on beige paper depicting the Fairfield-Suisun Air Force Base layout. A bold red line with cross markers traces a flight path from the upper right, down over the base, and off the bottom edge. Handwritten annotations in blue ink describe the object's movements. A red signature is visible at the bottom.

elements.description: A large, detailed architectural site plan or base layout map titled 'MILITARY CONSTRUCTION FAIRFIELD-SUISUN AIR FORCE BASE NEAR FAIRFIELD, CALIFORNIA BASIC LAYOUT PLAN', dated OCTOBER 1946. The map depicts the layout of an airfield including runways, taxiways, buildings, and a 'CONTROL TOWER' labeled '3'. A prominent red line is drawn across the map, annotated with numbered points (1, 2) and text describing the 'TRACK OF UNIDENTIFIED AERIAL OBJECT'. Text includes: 'OBJECT CAME INTO VIEW AT AN ALT. OF 500-1000 FEET TRAVELLING AT AN ESTIMATED SPEED OVER 400 MPH', 'OBJECT CLIMBED ON A LOW TRAJECTORY TO 1500 ALT.', 'OBJECT SLOWED TO AN ESTIMATED 200 MPH', 'OBJECT CLIMBED TO AN ESTIMATED 3000 FEET AND LEVELED OFF MOMENTARILY THEN STARTED TO CLIMB AGAIN', 'OBJECT MAINTAINED A FAST CLIMB TO 20,000 FEET', and 'OBJECT LOST FROM VIEW AT AN ESTIMATED 20,000 FEET ALT.'. The map also contains a signature at the bottom center and a 'CONFIDENTIAL' stamp.

A small circular diagram in the top right corner labeled 'SURFACE WINDROSE' with various wind direction indicators. To its left is a small inset map labeled 'VICINITY MAP' showing the location of Fairfield-Suisun AFB relative to surrounding areas.

A small inset map in the top left labeled 'AIRFIELD' showing the layout of runways. Below it is a section labeled 'LOCATION OF DIVERSION DAM', 'WATER WELLS & RADIO RANGE'. In the bottom left is a cross-section diagram labeled 'GASOLINE SUPPLY WHARF' with locations 'FAIRFIELD' and 'SUISUN' marked.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the main visual elements—a hand-drawn map of Fairfield-Suisun AFB with a prominent red line, annotations, and a signature—but Description B provides extensive specific quoted text and details (e.g., dates, object movement descriptions, inset diagrams) that Description A omits, without direct contradiction.
